programs.programName: Iowa Medicaid Home & Community Based Services Elderly Waiver

verified.notes: Income and asset limits from 2025 source; no 2026 official values found. Program active as of 2026 per state docs.

verified.notes: HCBS encompasses multiple waivers; Elderly waiver for seniors (65+) applies per senior-caregiver pathway. No income/assets found for seniors. New age-based waivers proposed for 2026 (Adults with Disabilities, Children and Youth) exclude seniors; current as of April 2026.

programs.programName: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)

verified.notes: No statewide main phone; multiple local PACE centers listed. No asset or income limits found in sources; PACE typically has no asset test and covers all eligible via Medicare/Medicaid.

programs.notes: No statewide main phone; multiple local PACE centers listed. No asset or income limits found in sources; PACE typically has no asset test and covers all eligible via Medicare/Medicaid.

verified.notes: No authoritative 2026 data found in search results for senior-specific age threshold, income limits, assets, or phone; SNAP has no senior age minimum or asset test per federal rules, but Iowa income limits unavailable here

programs.notes: No authoritative 2026 data found in search results for senior-specific age threshold, income limits, assets, or phone; SNAP has no senior age minimum or asset test per federal rules, but Iowa income limits unavailable here

verified.notes: Income limits based on 200% of 2025 federal poverty guidelines (FY26, Oct 1 2025–Sept 30 2026). Search results do not provide specific poverty guideline dollar amounts; conversion calculated from standard 2025 federal poverty line ($15,060 for individual, $19,720 for couple of 2). Phone number is IMPACT's energy assistance line for Boone, Jasper, Marion, Polk, Warren counties; statewide applications also accepted through local community action agencies and online portal at liheap-apply.hhs.iowa.gov. No age restriction found in eligibility criteria; program serves all qualifying low-income households, with early application window (Oct 1) for seniors 60+ and disabled persons. No asset limits specified in search results.

No age restriction found in eligibility criteria; program serves all qualifying low-income households, with early application window (Oct 1) for seniors 60+ and disabled persons. No asset limits specified in search results.

programs.programName: Senior Health Insurance Information Program (SHIIP) — Iowa's implementation of the national SHIP program

verified.notes: SHIIP is a free counseling service for Medicare beneficiaries with no age, income, or asset eligibility requirements

verified.notes: No single statewide Meals on Wheels program in Iowa; locally administered with varying eligibility (often 60+ for seniors, homebound). No official .gov source found for uniform criteria; no income/assets thresholds or central phone specified.
